root_text.an3.42:1.3: Sīlavantānaṁ dassanakāmo hoti, saddhammaṁ sotukāmo hoti, vigatamalamaccherena cetasā agāraṁ ajjhāvasati muttacāgo payatapāṇi vossaggarato yācayogo dānasaṁvibhāgarato.
root_text.an3.42:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, tīhi ṭhānehi saddho pasanno veditabbo.

translation_text.an3.42:1.1: “There are three grounds, mendicants, by which a person with faith and confidence can be known.
translation_text.an3.42:1.3: They like to see ethical people. They like to hear the true teaching. And they live at home rid of the stain of stinginess, freely generous, open-handed, loving to let go, committed to charity, loving to give and to share.
translation_text.an3.42:1.4: These are the three grounds by which a person with faith and confidence can be known.
translation_text.an3.42:2.1: They like to see ethical people;
translation_text.an3.42:2.2: they want to hear the true teaching;
translation_text.an3.42:2.3: they’ve driven out the stain of stinginess:
translation_text.an3.42:2.4: that’s who’s called an individual of faith.”
